incoming_email:
Subject: Change role from manager to agent

Hi,

Can you change rachel@orbitline.com from manager to agent? She should still access support@ but not see team performance analytics.
expected_reply:
Hi Chris,

A workspace admin can make that change from collabmail.com/settings/team. Select rachel@orbitline.com, change her role from Manager to Agent, and keep her assigned to the support@ shared inbox.

After the change, Rachel will still be able to view and reply to conversations in support@, use templates, add internal notes, and manage assigned work. She will no longer have manager-level analytics or team performance access.

If you are not an admin, please ask a workspace admin to make the update or confirm the request here.
generated_reply:
Hi there,

Happy to help with changing Rachel's role.

An admin on your CollabMail workspace can adjust user roles from the Team Settings page at `collabmail.com/settings/team`.
1.  Navigate to the Team Settings page.
2.  Find `rachel@orbitline.com` in the user list.
3.  Click on her current role (Manager) and select 'Agent' from the dropdown.
4.  Save the changes.

When Rachel's role is changed from Manager to Agent, she will still retain access to any shared inboxes she is assigned to, such as `support@`. The key change is that as an Agent, she will no longer have access to team performance analytics or workspace settings. Her permissions will be limited to viewing and replying in her assigned shared inboxes, using templates, and managing conversations.

Let me know if you have any trouble making this change or if you have other questions!

incoming_email:
Subject: Onboard HR department

Hi CollabMail,

Our HR team wants to start using people@company.com for candidate and employee questions. What steps should we take to onboard a new department without mixing HR emails into support@?
expected_reply:
Hi Laura,

Great question. To onboard HR separately, create a new shared inbox for people@company.com from collabmail.com/settings/inboxes and add only the HR team members who should access it.

Keep HR separate from support@ by using distinct inbox membership, tags, templates, and assignment rules. You can also restrict analytics access to HR managers if you do not want support leads seeing HR volume or response-time reports.

Before launch, connect the Gmail mailbox, create HR-specific templates, set assignment rules for candidate and employee categories, and test with a few internal messages. We can review the setup if you send us your desired HR user list and workflow.
generated_reply:
Hi there,

That's a great step to onboard your HR team with a dedicated inbox! You can definitely set up `people@company.com` to function completely independently, ensuring no HR emails get mixed into `support@`.

Here are the steps to onboard your HR department:

1.  **Create a New Shared Inbox for `people@company.com`:** The first step is to create a new shared inbox specifically for your HR department. This ensures that all emails sent to `people@company.com` will live in their own dedicated space, separate from `support@` or any other inbox.
2.  **Add Your HR Team as Agents:** Invite your HR team members to your CollabMail workspace and assign them as agents *only* to the `people@company.com` shared inbox. They will only see conversations and settings related to their inbox and will not have access to `support@` conversations unless explicitly added.
3.  **Configure HR-Specific Workflows:** Within the `people@company.com` inbox, you can set up assignment rules (e.g., round-robin for general inquiries, or specific assignments for particular HR specialists), create custom tags (like "onboarding", "benefits", "payroll"), and develop email templates tailored for common HR responses. Like setting up `support@`, it's often best to start with a simple set of rules and tags and add more complexity as your team identifies specific needs.
4.  **Assign HR Inbox Managers (Optional):** You can assign an HR lead as a manager for the `people@company.com` inbox. This grants them control over the specific inbox's settings, rules, and agents, without giving them full workspace admin privileges or access to other department's inboxes.

By following these steps, you'll create a completely separate and secure environment for your HR team's communications, ensuring their emails remain private and distinct from other departments.

Let us know if you'd like a more detailed guide or have any specific questions about setting up their initial workflows!

incoming_email:
Subject: Password reset not arriving

Hi,

I requested a password reset twice but never received the email. I checked spam. My login is kara@northpeak.io.
expected_reply:
Hi Kara,

Sorry the reset email is not reaching you. I checked the account status for kara@northpeak.io, and the user appears active.

Please make sure your mail team allows messages from no-reply@collabmail.com and try the reset again at collabmail.com/reset-password. If your workspace uses Google SSO, you will not receive a CollabMail password reset and should sign in with Google instead.

If the email still does not arrive within 10 minutes, ask your admin to confirm whether SSO is enforced for your workspace, and we can check the delivery logs for the reset message.
generated_reply:
Hi Kara,

Sorry to hear you're having trouble receiving your password reset emails, especially after checking your spam folder. That can definitely be frustrating when you're trying to get logged in.

Sometimes these emails can be delayed or caught by less common inbox filters. Could you also check your 'Promotions' or 'Other' folders in case it landed there? It might also help to add `noreply@collabmail.com` to your safe sender list or contacts to ensure future emails aren't blocked.

Alternatively, if your CollabMail workspace has an administrator, they can initiate a password reset for your account directly from their admin dashboard, which might bypass any email delivery issues you're experiencing.

If these steps don't resolve the issue, please let us know your CollabMail workspace name and the approximate time you last requested a reset. We can then check our authentication and email logs for `kara@northpeak.io` to pinpoint why the reset isn't arriving.

generated_reply:

incoming_email:

expected_reply:

Once payment is completed, all billing banners will clear automatically. If AP needs vendor forms or a W-9, reply here and we'll send them right away.
generated_reply:
Hi there,

Sorry to hear your team is experiencing a service interruption and cannot access your CollabMail dashboard due to an overdue invoice.

When an invoice becomes overdue, account access is automatically suspended. To quickly restore access for your team, the fastest path is to update your payment method. We understand that changes in AP systems, like yours, can sometimes cause brief delays in processing.

You can update your payment details directly from your billing portal at collabmail.com/billing/payment-methods. Please ensure you are logged in as a workspace owner or a billing-only contact with payment modification permissions.

Once your payment has been successfully updated and processed, your account will automatically re-activate, and your team will regain full access to all CollabMail features, including the analytics dashboard.

If you have already updated your payment information and need immediate re-activation, please reply to this email, and we can check the status and expedite the unlock on our end.

incoming_email:
Subject: Upgrade only analytics users?

Hi,

We have 55 agents using shared inboxes, but only 5 managers need the advanced analytics dashboard. Do we have to upgrade everyone to Business, or can just those 5 users be Business?
expected_reply:
Hi Carla,

CollabMail plans are applied at the workspace level, so all active seats in a workspace must be on the same plan. That means upgrading to Business would move all 55 users to Business, not only the 5 managers.

If advanced analytics is only needed by managers, there are two options. You can upgrade the full workspace so managers get dashboard access across all shared inboxes, or we can discuss an Enterprise arrangement with custom reporting access and volume pricing.

For 55 seats, Business would be $2,695 per month before tax at $49 per user. If you would like an Enterprise quote, we can schedule a pricing review at collabmail.com/enterprise-demo.
generated_reply:
Hi there,

Thanks for asking about upgrading specific users for advanced analytics. I understand you're looking to provide your 5 managers with higher-tier features without upgrading your entire team of 55 agents, which makes a lot of sense for cost efficiency.

In CollabMail, subscription plans apply at the workspace level, not on a per-user basis. This means that to enable features like the advanced analytics dashboard, which is typically part of our Business plan, the entire workspace must be on that plan.

We don't currently support a mixed-plan setup where a subset of users within the same workspace can be on a different subscription tier. So, to provide those 5 managers with access to the advanced analytics dashboard, all 55 users in your workspace would need to be on the Business plan.

While this doesn't allow for granular feature upgrades per user, it ensures all members of your team benefit from the full set of features available on your chosen plan. If you'd like to explore the full benefits of the Business plan for your entire team, or discuss specific features, please don't hesitate to reply. We're happy to walk through it with you.

incoming_email:
Subject: We were charged for inactive users

Our June invoice includes 33 users, but only 29 people are active in CollabMail. Four people left the company in May and no longer access support@ or sales@. Why were we billed for them?
expected_reply:
Hi Lena,

Thanks for flagging this. CollabMail bills for provisioned seats, not only users who actively log in during the month. I checked your workspace and the four former employees were suspended in Google Workspace but were still listed as active CollabMail seats until June 18.

I've removed those four seats from future billing. Because they were not using the shared inboxes after May 31, I can apply a goodwill credit of $116 for the June seat charges to your next invoice.

To avoid this later, we recommend enabling seat sync at collabmail.com/settings/team so suspended Google users are automatically removed from CollabMail after 24 hours.
generated_reply:
Hi there,

Thanks for reaching out about your June invoice. I understand your concern about being billed for users who are no longer active in CollabMail.

Our billing system calculates charges based on the number of active user seats provisioned in your workspace at the time of invoicing. If a user leaves your company, they continue to occupy a seat and incur charges until they are manually removed from your CollabMail workspace by an administrator. This is why the four individuals who left in May were still included on your June invoice.

To ensure you are only billed for active users, an administrator can remove inactive users from your workspace at any time via **collabmail.com/settings/users**. Removing users will update your seat count for the next billing cycle.

Regarding your June invoice, I can submit a request to review for a partial refund for the four inactive users for the period they were billed after their departure in May. Please confirm if you would like me to proceed with this review.

Let me know if you have any other questions!

expected_reply:

That rebrand workflow makes complete sense. Bulk editing template footers or using shared snippets would save a lot of manual work and reduce inconsistencies.

generated_reply:
Hi there,

Currently, CollabMail does not have a built-in feature for bulk editing template content or a shared snippet functionality that would automatically update across multiple templates when the source snippet changes. This means that each template's footer will need to be updated individually.

You can manage this by going to `collabmail.com/settings/templates`, where you can open each template, update the footer content, and save your changes.

We appreciate you bringing up the idea of bulk editing and shared snippets for templates. This is valuable feedback, and I will be sure to pass it along to our product team for consideration in future developments. We are always looking for ways to improve the user experience.

Please let us know if you have any other questions while you're working through these updates.

incoming_email:
Subject: Can agencies use CollabMail for multiple clients?

Hi,

We are an agency managing customer support for five ecommerce brands. Can we keep each client's shared inbox, templates, and analytics separate?
expected_reply:
Hi Elena,

Yes, agencies can use CollabMail, and the best setup is usually separate workspaces or clearly separated shared inboxes depending on how strict the client separation needs to be.

If agents work across all five brands but data must remain separate for reporting and permissions, separate workspaces provide the cleanest boundary. If the same team can access every brand, one workspace with five shared inboxes, client-specific templates, tags, and analytics filters may be enough.

We'd be happy to review your access requirements and recommend the structure that avoids mixing client conversations.
generated_reply:
Hi there,

It's great to hear you're looking to use CollabMail to manage support for your ecommerce clients! We often work with agencies who need to keep client operations distinct and separate.

CollabMail is well-suited for this kind of setup, and the best way to achieve full separation for each client is by setting up a dedicated **workspace** for each one.

Here’s how that addresses your needs:
*   **Client Shared Inboxes:** Each client's unique shared inboxes (e.g., support@clientA.com, help@clientB.com) would reside within their dedicated workspace, keeping their email threads completely separate.
*   **Templates:** Templates are specific to each workspace. This means client A's templates will not be accessible or mixed with client B's, ensuring brand consistency and correct messaging for each.
*   **Analytics:** All analytics and reporting are generated at the workspace level. You'll get clear, isolated insights into each client's performance without any data overlap.

Your agents can be members of multiple client workspaces using a single login and easily switch between clients. While each workspace is typically billed independently based on its users and plan, we can also discuss consolidated billing options for agencies managing several clients, especially on our Business or Enterprise plans.

To help you get started, I recommend setting up a trial workspace for one of your clients so you can see firsthand how the structure works. We can then discuss the best approach for onboarding your additional clients and any specific billing needs you might have.

incoming_email:
Subject: Migration from Hiver

Hello,

We are considering moving from Hiver to CollabMail for 35 support and success users. Can you migrate shared inbox history, tags, and templates?
expected_reply:
Hi Caroline,

Yes, we can help teams migrate from Hiver. The typical migration includes setting up the same Gmail shared inboxes, recreating tags, importing or rebuilding email templates, and advising on assignment rules.

Historical conversation migration depends on what data you can export from Hiver and what you want visible inside CollabMail. Many teams keep older history in Gmail and start fresh with CollabMail assignments and analytics from the migration date.

If you send us the number of shared inboxes, template count, tag list, and desired launch date, we can provide a migration checklist and timeline for your 35-user team.
generated_reply:
Hello,

Thanks for reaching out and considering CollabMail for your team's migration from Hiver! We understand that moving platforms involves careful planning, and we're here to help.

Regarding your questions about migrating data:

*   **Shared Inbox History:** We can typically import historical emails into your CollabMail shared inboxes. These will appear as new conversations, preserving the original sender, recipient, and timestamps depending on the export format from Hiver. While we can bring over the email content, direct migration of Hiver-specific internal notes, assignments, and granular conversation states usually requires a custom approach or may not transfer with full fidelity. We'd need to review Hiver's export capabilities to provide more precise details.
*   **Tags:** Yes, we can help with migrating your tags. This often involves reviewing the tag data exported from Hiver and then either bulk creating them in CollabMail or mapping them to new categories within your workspace.
*   **Templates:** Absolutely, migrating your existing response templates is straightforward. You can easily copy and paste the content from Hiver into CollabMail's template editor.

To give you the most accurate plan for your specific Hiver setup and data, we recommend scheduling a call with one of our migration specialists. They can walk you through the process, discuss Hiver's export options, and outline the best strategy for bringing your data into CollabMail. Please reply to this email or visit collabmail.com/migration-consult to book a session.

We look forward to helping you make a smooth transition!

incoming_email:

Hi support,

The analytics dashboard loads as a blank white page for everyone in our workspace. We need yesterday's response time report for an exec meeting in 40 minutes.
expected_reply:
Hi Danielle,

I'm sorry this is happening right before your meeting. A blank analytics page across the whole workspace usually points to a browser script issue or a dashboard service error.

Please try opening collabmail.com/analytics in a private window and disable browser extensions such as ad blockers for CollabMail. While you test that, I've started checking your workspace dashboard logs.

As a workaround, an admin can export yesterday's response-time report from collabmail.com/analytics/export if the dashboard shell is unavailable. If that export also fails, reply with the date range and shared inboxes you need, and we'll generate the CSV for support@ and send it to the workspace owner.
generated_reply:
Hello,

I understand how critical it is to access your analytics, especially with an exec meeting coming up in 40 minutes.

For your immediate need to get yesterday's response time report, you can export the data directly from collabmail.com/analytics/export. This should allow you to get the report you need for your meeting while we investigate the dashboard issue.

A blank analytics dashboard for everyone typically points to a browser-specific issue or a temporary caching problem. Please ask your team to try these steps:

1.  Clear their browser's cache and cookies.
2.  Try accessing the dashboard in an incognito/private browsing window.
3.  Temporarily disable any browser extensions that might be interfering.
4.  Attempt to access the dashboard using a different browser (e.g., Chrome if they are on Firefox, or vice versa).

If the dashboard remains blank after trying these steps, please let us know the browser and version you are using, and if you see any errors in your browser's developer console (usually opened by pressing F12 or Cmd+Option+I). We'll help get this resolved for you quickly.

incoming_email:
Subject: Internal notes disappearing?

Hi,

One agent says her internal note disappeared from a customer conversation. We rely on notes for handoffs, so this is worrying. Can notes be deleted automatically?
expected_reply:
Hi Peter,

Internal notes are not deleted automatically by CollabMail. They can be edited or deleted by users with the right permissions, and those changes appear in the conversation activity log.

Please open the affected conversation and click Activity. You should see whether the note was created, edited, deleted, or if the user wrote it on a different conversation in the thread. If you are an admin, you can also check collabmail.com/settings/audit-log for note deletion events.

Send us the conversation URL and the approximate time the note was written, and we'll help trace what happened.
generated_reply:
Hi there,

I understand how concerning it is when internal notes, which are so critical for handoffs, appear to go missing. That's definitely something we want to investigate thoroughly.

To answer your primary question, CollabMail internal notes cannot be deleted automatically. They are designed to be a persistent part of the conversation history for exactly the reasons you mentioned, like handoffs and context.

It's possible the agent might have drafted a note but navigated away before posting it, or perhaps posted it in a different conversation by mistake. Sometimes, refreshing the browser or clearing the cache can also resolve minor display issues, though notes are generally very stable.

To help us understand what happened, could you please provide us with a few more details?

*   The URL or ID of the specific customer conversation where the note disappeared.
*   The name of the agent who created the note.
*   A rough timestamp (date and time) when the note was supposedly created.
*   Any part of the note's content that the agent remembers.

With this information, we can check our system logs and trace the activity to see if the note was posted, when, and by whom. We'll do our best to help locate the missing note and ensure this doesn't happen again.

Hi there,

Thanks for letting us know about the trouble you're experiencing with the iOS app crashing when opening email templates. I understand how frustrating it can be when a core feature isn't working as expected, especially on mobile, and appreciate you providing your device, iOS, and app versions.

Given that the desktop version works fine, this often points to a local app data issue on the mobile device. Could you please try the following steps on your iPhone 14:

1.  **Fully close the CollabMail app** from your app switcher.
2.  **Delete and reinstall the CollabMail app** from the App Store. This will ensure you have a fresh installation of version 4.7.2 and clears any cached data that might be causing the crash.
3.  **Log back into your account.**

In the meantime, you can continue to access and use your email templates without issue on the desktop version of CollabMail.

If the crashes continue after reinstalling, please let us know the exact timestamp of the next crash and confirm if it happens with all templates or only specific ones. This additional detail will help our technical team investigate the crash logs more effectively.

We're here to help get this resolved for you.

expected_reply:
Hi Noor,

Thanks for checking the Android notification setting already. Since email notifications still arrive, your mention settings are likely correct and the issue is probably the mobile push token.

Please open the CollabMail Android app, go to Settings, sign out, then sign back in. That refreshes the device push token. Also confirm you are on the latest app version from Google Play and that battery optimization is not restricting CollabMail in Android settings.

If push notifications still do not work, send us your device model, Android version, and the time of a recent mention. We'll check whether the push event was generated and delivered to Google Firebase.
generated_reply:
Hi there,

I'm sorry to hear your Android push notifications for mentions have stopped working. Since email notifications are still arriving, this likely points to a setting specific to your mobile app or device rather than the core mention functionality.

First, please check your personal notification settings on the web at collabmail.com/settings/notifications and confirm that 'Mobile Push Notifications' are enabled for 'Mentions'.

If those settings are correct, sometimes the push notification token for the app can become stale. Please try these steps on your Android device:
1.  **Force stop the CollabMail app:** Go to your Android device's Settings > Apps > CollabMail > Force Stop.
2.  **Clear the app's cache:** From the same CollabMail app settings page, go to Storage > Clear Cache.
3.  **Re-open the CollabMail app.**

These steps often refresh the app's connection to our push notification service. If mentions still don't trigger push notifications after trying the above, please send us your Android device model, Android OS version, and the CollabMail app version. We can then review our push notification logs for your device to identify any specific errors.
